An investigation has been launched into the circumstances of aCentral Westhighway crash on Tuesday.

Loginor signup to continue reading

Paramedics from NSW Ambulance Service were called to the Olympic Highway near Crowther - About 40km north of Young - at around 2.30pm on August 6.

Once at the scene, they discovered a truck had crashed into a tree.

The driver, a 40-year-old man, was trapped before being freed by Fire and Rescue NSW. By the time paramedics arrived, the man had been freed from the truck.

A spokesman for NSW Ambulance Service described the incident as a "single patient motor vehicle crash."

Paramedics assisted the 40-year-old who was then airlifted to Canberra Hospital.

NSW Police were also called to the scene of the crash.

A spokesman confirmed that an investigation into the circumstances surrounding the crashhad been launched.

title: Truck driver airlifted after crash on Olympic Highway | Central Western Daily | Orange, NSW
description: Truck driver airlifted to hospital after crashing into a tree on the Olympic Highway near Crowther. Road closed, then partially reopened.
